phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest crude oil supply and production data. ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Fetch and report: - OPEC+ latest production decision: current output quota, any announced cuts or increases, and the next scheduled OPEC+ meeting date - US EIA Weekly Crude Oil Inventories: most recent Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (state whether it was an Inventory Build or Inventory Draw, and by how many barrels) - API Weekly Crude Oil Stock Change: most recent Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (confirm alignment or divergence with EIA) - US Baker Hughes Weekly Rig Count: most recent Actual vs Previous — is the US active rig count rising (supply risk) or falling (supply tightening)? - Any major OPEC+ member (Saudi Arabia, Russia, UAE, Iraq) unilateral production announcements in the last 7 days - Any supply outages or force majeure events affecting production (e.g., Libya, Nigeria, Kazakhstan) ⚠ STRICT ANTI-HALLUCINATION RULE: Verify all Actual vs Forecast vs Previous figures from reliable financial calendars (Investing.com, EIA.gov, Baker Hughes website, Reuters). Do NOT estimate or fabricate figures. If data is unavailable, state "Data Unavailable". Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - OPEC+ Quota: [current mb/d target] | Latest Decision: [cut/hold/increase + detail] | Next Meeting: [date] - EIA Inventory: [Actual] vs Forecast [F] vs Prev [P] | Result: [Build/Draw of X million barrels] - API Inventory: [Actual] vs Forecast [F] vs Prev [P] | EIA/API Alignment: [Confirming/Diverging] - Baker Hughes Rig Count: [current] vs Previous [P] | Trend: [Rising/Falling/Flat] - Supply Tightness Rating: [Very Tight / Tight / Balanced / Oversupplied] - Supply Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - SUPPLY & PRODUCTION DYNAMICS] - OPEC+ Stance: [cut/hold/increase + quota mb/d + next meeting date] - EIA Inventory: [Actual vs Forecast | Build/Draw of X mb] - API Inventory: [Actual vs Forecast | confirmation or divergence] - Rig Count: [current vs previous | trend: Rising/Falling] - Supply Tightness: [Very Tight / Tight / Balanced / Oversupplied] - Key Supply Event: [most important supply-side headline in last 7 days, or None] - Core Logic: [why this supply picture is bullish, bearish, or neutral for Brent today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

phase3:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest geopolitical risks and supply disruption threats affecting global crude oil markets. Fetch and report: - Middle East tensions: any escalation or de-escalation in the last 7 days involving Iran, Israel, Iraq, Yemen (Houthis), or Saudi Arabia that could threaten production or shipping lanes (specifically the Strait of Hormuz — ~20% of global oil transit) - Red Sea / Suez Canal shipping disruptions: any Houthi attacks on tankers, route diversions via Cape of Good Hope (adds ~14 days and cost to shipping), or changes in tanker insurance premiums - Russian oil sanctions: any new EU/G7 sanctions, price cap enforcement updates, or shadow fleet seizures affecting Russian crude exports (Russia ~10–11 mb/d output) - Other key supply chain threats: Libya civil unrest, Nigeria pipeline outages, Kazakhstan Tengiz output issues, or any other OPEC member supply disruption - Any immediate diplomatic breakthrough or ceasefire that could REDUCE the geopolitical risk premium (bearish for oil prices) ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - Middle East Risk: [Escalating/Stable/De-escalating] | Strait of Hormuz Threat: [Active/Low] | Detail: [one line] - Red Sea Disruption: [Active/Reduced/Resolved] | Tanker Rerouting: [Yes/No] | Supply Impact: [mb/d affected or None] - Russia Sanctions: [Tightening/Stable/Loosening] | Estimated Supply Impact: [mb/d] - Other Disruptions: [detail or None] - Geopolitical Risk Premium: [High / Medium / Low / None] - Safe-Haven Oil Demand: [Active/Neutral/Absent] - Geopolitical Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - GEOPOLITICAL RISK & DISRUPTIONS] - Middle East: [Escalating/Stable/De-escalating | key detail] - Red Sea/Shipping: [Active disruption / Reduced / Resolved | tanker rerouting: Yes/No] - Russia Sanctions: [Tightening/Stable | estimated mb/d impact] - Other Supply Threats: [detail or None] - Geopolitical Premium: [High / Medium / Low / None] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [how geopolitical risks are adding or reducing the risk premium on Brent today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```
phase4: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest US Dollar and macroeconomic data relevant to crude oil pricing. Fetch and report: - DXY (US Dollar Index): current level, today's directional move (Up/Down X%), and the 5-day trend. Note: Oil is priced in USD — a stronger DXY makes oil MORE expensive for non-USD buyers, suppressing demand (bearish for oil). A weaker DXY makes oil CHEAPER globally, supporting demand (bullish for oil). - Federal Reserve interest rate stance: current Fed Funds Rate, next FOMC meeting rate-cut probability from CME FedWatch (exact %), and any recent Fed speaker comments in the last 48 hours. Note: High interest rates suppress economic growth and oil demand; rate-cut signals are bullish for oil via risk-on flows and demand expectations. - US Recession Risk indicators: US Yield Curve status (inverted/normalising/steepening), latest US Unemployment data vs Forecast, and any major investment bank recession probability forecasts (Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan — state % probability if available) - Risk Asset Correlation: S&P 500 direction today and VIX level — oil typically moves with risk-on/risk-off sentiment ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. phase5: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest CFTC COT positioning data and Brent crude oil futures curve structure. Fetch and report: - CFTC Commitments of Traders (COT) Report — most recent release: NET Non-Commercial (speculative / managed money) positioning for Brent Crude (ICE) and/or WTI Crude (NYMEX) — exact net long or net short contract count. Is positioning at a historical extreme (very crowded long or short)? Use this for identifying the macro trend direction and whether smart money is adding or reducing exposure. - Futures Curve Structure: identify whether the Brent futures curve is in BACKWARDATION or CONTANGO — state the exact spread between the prompt month (front month, M1) and the next month (M2) in USD/barrel. Explain the implication: Backwardation (M1 > M2) signals tight physical supply and immediate demand — bullish. Contango (M1 < M2) signals oversupply or weak spot demand — bearish/neutral. - Brent-WTI Spread: current spread in USD/barrel — widening or narrowing? What does this signal about relative demand/supply between European and US markets? - Open Interest trend: is total open interest in Brent futures rising (new money entering, trend confirmation) or falling (position liquidation)? - Any notable options market signal: large put or call positioning, unusual options activity, or any dealer hedging flow that suggests directional bias ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. [Yes/No] | Smart Money Trend: [Adding Longs/Reducing Longs/Adding Shorts] - Futures Curve: [Backwardation/Contango] | M1–M2 Spread: [USD/bbl] | Physical Market Signal: [Tight/Balanced/Oversupplied] - Brent-WTI Spread: [USD/bbl] | Trend: [Widening/Narrowing] | Signal: [brief implication] - Open Interest: [Rising/Falling] | Trend Confirmation: [Yes/No] - Market Structure Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P5 - MARKET STRUCTURE & SENTIMENT] - CFTC Net Position (Brent/WTI): [net contracts + long/short | extreme: Yes/No | trend: Adding/Reducing] - Futures Curve: [Backwardation/Contango | M1–M2 spread: USD/bbl | physical signal: Tight/Balanced/Oversupplied] - Brent-WTI Spread: [USD/bbl | Widening/Narrowing] - Open Interest: [Rising/Falling | trend confirmation: Yes/No] - Core Logic: [what smart money positioning + futures curve structure implies for Brent direction today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```
phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear UKOIL (Brent Crude) institutional intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Supply & Production Dynamics: [P1_OUTPUT]
2. Global Demand Outlook: [P2_OUTPUT]
3. Geopolitical Risk & Disruptions: [P3_OUTPUT]
4. USD Correlation & Macro Constraints: [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Market Structure & Sentiment (COT + Futures Curve): [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict, lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If all factors align, confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled events and session liquidity (note key times: EIA Inventory release 15:30 UTC Wed, Baker Hughes Rig Count 17:00 UTC Fri, London open 07:00–08:00 UTC, NY open 13:00–14:00 UTC).

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60% to dominant pillar | ~10% each to others]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No]
- Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts — reason]
- Contradiction Check: [list any conflicting signals, or "None — all factors aligned"]
- Invalidation Point: [what specific event or data shift would flip this bias]
- Optimal Trading Window: [e.g., session + time UTC + reason, e.g., "Wait for EIA release at 15:30 UTC — high volatility event"]

Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Steps 2.2 and 2.3):
```
[UKOIL BRENT MASTER BIAS - [CURRENT_DATE]]
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10]
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [factor + logic]
- Primary Driver 2: [factor + logic]
- Dominant Theme: [label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [dominant pillar + ~60%]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes / No]
- Market Regime: [label]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts]
- Contradiction: [summary or "None"]
- Invalidation: [specific event or data release that would flip the bias]
- Optimal Trading Window: [session + time UTC + reason]
```

phase8_blog_html: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Act as an expert Commodities Analyst and Financial Blogger. Using the research data below, write a comprehensive, highly detailed blog post explaining today's UKOIL (Brent Crude) fundamental bias so that anyone reading it can fully understand the global oil market conditions.
